description: Contradictory data: chief complaint describes life-threatening anaphylaxis but vitals are entirely normal. Tests whether the agent over-weights one stream.

patient.name: Rebecca H., 32F
patient.chief_complaint: Anaphylaxis after eating shellfish 20 minutes ago — diffuse hives, throat tightness, wheezing, lip swelling. Used EpiPen 5 minutes ago and feels significantly improved. Husband driving her in.
patient.history: Known shellfish allergy with prior anaphylactic reactions, carries EpiPen

rubric_notes: Anaphylaxis with EpiPen response can show normal vitals AFTER epi (which is why the vitals look benign here). BUT biphasic reactions occur in ~20% of anaphylaxis cases at 1-8 hour mark. Per RUBRIC §3 anaphylaxis = ESI 1 mandate regardless of post-epi vital response. Agent that classifies on vitals alone (ESI 3-4 because vitals are normal) misses biphasic reaction risk. Strict scoring: ESI 2 or 1 only. Patient needs observation, IM/IV access, second dose epi available, steroids + antihistamines.
